overview.short: Nhavigad is also called as Nhavagad or Ratangad.
overview.description: Nhavigad is also called as Nhavagad or Ratangad. It is located in the Baglan area of Nashik district. There are two main hill ranges in Baglan area the Selbari range and Dholbari range. These two ranges run parallel to each other in east-west direction.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mangi-Tungi
topPlaces.description: Mangi-Tungi is a prominent twin-pinnacled peak and Digambar Jain Pilgrimage Site, located near Tahrabad about 125 km from Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Mangi, 4,343 ft (1,324 m) high above sea level, is the western pinnacle and Tungi, 4,366 ft (1,331 m) high, the eastern.

topPlaces.name: Statue of Ahimsa
topPlaces.description: The Statue of Ahimsa is located at Mangi-Tungi, in Nashik, in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is the tallest Jain statue in the world.

topPlaces.name: Haranbari Dam
topPlaces.description: Haranbari Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Mosam River near Satana, Nashik District in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Selbari Range
topPlaces.description: Selbari is a mountain range located in Nashik District in Maharashtra, India. Spanning east-west the hills form the northernmost extent of the Sahyadris and serve as a natural boundary between Nashik District and Dhule district.

topPlaces.name: Mulher
topPlaces.description: Mulher is a village in the Nashik district of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Hargad Fort
topPlaces.description: Hargad Fort is located in the Baglan area of Nashik district. There are two main hill ranges in Baglan area, the Selbari range is south of the Dolbari hill range.

items.note: Mangi-Tungi is a prominent twin-pinnacled peak and Digambar Jain Pilgrimage Site, located near Tahrabad about 125 km from Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Mangi, 4,343 ft (1,324 m) high above sea level, is the western pinnacle and Tungi, 4,366 ft (1,331 m) high, the eastern.
The Statue of Ahimsa is located at Mangi-Tungi, in Nashik, in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is the tallest Jain statue in the world.
Haranbari Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Mosam River near Satana, Nashik District in the state of Maharashtra in India.

Selbari is a mountain range located in Nashik District in Maharashtra, India. Spanning east-west the hills form the northernmost extent of the Sahyadris and serve as a natural boundary between Nashik District and Dhule district.
Mulher is a village in the Nashik district of Maharashtra, India.
Hargad Fort is located in the Baglan area of Nashik district. There are two main hill ranges in Baglan area, the Selbari range is south of the Dolbari hill range.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nhavigad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nhavigad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (132 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (101 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nhavigad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nhavigad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nhavigad?
faq.a: Stays in Nhavigad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nhavigad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mangi-Tungi, Statue of Ahimsa, Haranbari Dam, Selbari Range, Mulher.
